mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized capacity expansion meeting robust demand, which expands corporate margins and earnings visibility, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment where the federal funds rate remains below 0.25 percent, equity markets price in prolonged liquidity support and suppressed bond yields, causing the S&P 500 to generate positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns average below zero when FEDFUNDS stays under 0.25%, or the signal's hit rate falls below 48% across two consecutive monetary policy cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS levels reflect sustained consumer expenditure growth, which directly translates to higher corporate top-line revenue and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days without requiring additional macroeconomic thresholds.
failure_condition: The series exhibits a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or predictive R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production increases by more than 1.0 index point over a three-month window, it signals overheating capacity that triggers anticipated monetary tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery that reduces wage-price pressures while expanding aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns, or the combined signal produces a directional accuracy below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized signals a transition from recessionary contraction to sustainable expansion, which improves corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns average below zero when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over any 4-year rolling window.
